title: NGE Abriged wiped from youtube
wikitext:
[]: NGE Abriged wiped from youtube             Quote Post by Jayfive » Thu Jan 27, 2011 8:56 am 'YouTube account Konataproduction has been terminated because we have received multiple third-party notifications of copyright infringement from claimants, including: * BandaiChannel' Somebody sit these people down and explain to them what a parody is.

[]: Re: NGE Abriged wiped from youtube             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Thu Jan 27, 2011 9:12 am Jayfive wrote: Somebody sit these people down and explain to them what a parody is.

[]:             Quote Post by Jayfive » Thu Jan 27, 2011 9:25 am It's not protected under parody law

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Thu Jan 27, 2011 10:11 am Jayfive wrote: Why not?

[]:             Quote Post by Jayfive » Thu Jan 27, 2011 12:02 pm Right, with you.

[]:             Quote Post by Clover » Thu Jan 27, 2011 1:28 pm Never stopped Little Kuriboh. Total bro kept re-uploading.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Thu Jan 27, 2011 1:50 pm Clover wrote: Never stopped Little Kuriboh. Total bro kept re-uploading.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Thu Jan 27, 2011 2:41 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: But you'll notice that they don't sell DVDs. If they tried, you bet your ass they would get smacked hard.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Thu Jan 27, 2011 3:21 pm NAveryW wrote: Yet they do sell merchandise. Immoral in my opinion, but they avoid putting characters obviously on what they're selling.

[]:             Quote Post by C.A.P. » Thu Jan 27, 2011 4:16 pm Oh boy, this argument. If I get a dollar every time this has been brought up, I would actually start my own quicker. You know, just for amusement, here's an abridger explaining her position on copyright laws. If anyone wants to know who this is and wants a source, let me know. While you're right, this is also a bit of a different situation. People in the mainstream are usually making money off their endeavor. So long as it's not for profit, not hurting their profits, and there is a significant portion changed, then it's legal, : )

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Thu Jan 27, 2011 4:42 pm Really it's a Schrodinger's cat sort of situation since there's isn't yet a legal precedent either way.

[]:             Quote Post by GasmaskAvenger » Thu Jan 27, 2011 7:57 pm sounds like he was removed not because of EVA, but because of... something else SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by Jayfive » Thu Jan 27, 2011 9:05 pm sounds like he was removed not because of EVA, but because of...something else

[]:             Quote Post by Clover » Thu Jan 27, 2011 9:50 pm Jayfive wrote: Pound for pound its better than ReDeath if you ask me.

[]:             Quote Post by C.A.P. » Thu Jan 27, 2011 10:28 pm Clover wrote: Don't even have to watch it to know that's bullshit. You know it too.

[]: My funnybone still wakes up in the night, screaming...             Quote Post by Killer Bee » Fri Jan 28, 2011 12:39 am I watched the first few episodes of NGE Abridged a while ago and found it painfully unfunny. Does it get better as it goes along?

[]:             Quote Post by GasmaskAvenger » Fri Jan 28, 2011 12:54 am on topic: is this that one abridged series where Rei had a cookie monster growl as her voice?

[]: Re: My funnybone still wakes up in the night, screaming...             Quote Post by NemZ » Fri Jan 28, 2011 1:23 am Killer Bee wrote: I watched the first few episodes of NGE Abridged a while ago and found it painfully unfunny. Does it get better as it goes along?

[]:             Quote Post by Jayfive » Fri Jan 28, 2011 7:39 am on topic: is this that one abridged series where Rei had a cookie monster growl as her voice?

[]:             Quote Post by C.A.P. » Fri Jan 28, 2011 8:15 am I watched the first episode recently before they got purged, and it was...okay. Voices serve their purposes, there were some funny jokes here and there, but there were some jokes where it's obvious they were trying too hard to get a laugh, and that's never a good sign with abridged series. Also, I can't help but think there's a little smugness in this series, but again, I've only seen the first episode in recent memory, so I don't know if it gets more obvious as it goes by. From what I've seen, it's just another run-of-the-mill attempt to put comedy into Evangelion, and if you put class clown comedy into Evangelion, then you're pretty much shooting yourself in the foot; what you're trying to accomplish, I have no clue. But what I want to know is why they used Rebulid footage for the first six episodes. Just becuase it looks better doesn't mean you should use it.
